root_text.an10.11:1.1: “Pañcaṅgasamannāgato, bhikkhave, bhikkhu pañcaṅgasamannāgataṁ senāsanaṁ sevamāno bhajamāno nacirasseva āsavānaṁ khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja vihareyya.
root_text.an10.11:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu saddho hoti; saddahati tathāgatassa bodhiṁ:
root_text.an10.11:2.4: appābādho hoti appātaṅko, samavepākiniyā gahaṇiyā samannāgato nātisītāya nāccuṇhāya majjhimāya padhānakkhamāya;
root_text.an10.11:2.5: asaṭho hoti amāyāvī, yathābhūtaṁ attānaṁ āvikattā satthari vā viññūsu vā sabrahmacārīsu;
root_text.an10.11:2.6: āraddhavīriyo viharati, akusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ pahānāya, kusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ upasampadāya; thāmavā daḷhaparakkamo anikkhittadhuro kusalesu dhammesu;
root_text.an10.11:2.7: paññavā hoti, udayatthagāminiyā paññāya samannāgato ariyāya nibbedhikāya sammā dukkhakkhayagāminiyā.
root_text.an10.11:2.8: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu pañcaṅgasamannāgato hoti.
root_text.an10.11:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, senāsanaṁ nātidūraṁ hoti nāccāsannaṁ gamanāgamanasampannaṁ
root_text.an10.11:3.5: tasmiṁ kho pana senāsane viharantassa appakasirena uppajjanti cīvarapiṇḍapātasenāsanagilānapaccayabhesajjaparikkhārā;
root_text.an10.11:3.6: tasmiṁ kho pana senāsane therā bhikkhū viharanti bahussutā āgatāgamā dhammadharā vinayadharā mātikādharā;
root_text.an10.11:3.7: te kālena kālaṁ upasaṅkamitvā paripucchati paripañhati:
root_text.an10.11:3.9: tassa te āyasmanto avivaṭañceva vivaranti anuttānīkatañca uttāniṁ karonti anekavihitesu ca kaṅkhāṭhāniyesu dhammesu kaṅkhaṁ paṭivinodenti.
root_text.an10.11:3.10: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, senāsanaṁ pañcaṅgasamannāgataṁ hoti.
root_text.an10.11:3.11: Pañcaṅgasamannāgato kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu pañcaṅgasamannāgataṁ senāsanaṁ sevamāno bhajamāno nacirasseva āsavānaṁ khayā …pe… sacchikatvā upasampajja vihareyyā”ti.

translation_text.an10.11:1.1: “Mendicants, a mendicant with five factors, using and frequenting lodgings with five factors, will soon realize the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life, and live having realized it with their own insight due to the ending of defilements.
translation_text.an10.11:2.1: And how does a mendicant have five factors?
translation_text.an10.11:2.2: It’s when a noble disciple has faith in the Realized One’s awakening:
translation_text.an10.11:2.3: ‘That Blessed One is perfected, a fully awakened Buddha, accomplished in knowledge and conduct, holy, knower of the world, supreme guide for those fit for training, teacher of gods and humans, awakened, blessed.’
translation_text.an10.11:2.4: They are rarely ill or unwell. Their stomach digests well, being neither too hot nor too cold, but just right, and fit for meditation.
translation_text.an10.11:2.5: They’re not devious or deceitful. They reveal themselves honestly to the Teacher or sensible spiritual companions.
translation_text.an10.11:2.6: They live with energy roused up for giving up unskillful qualities and embracing skillful qualities. They’re strong, staunchly vigorous, not slacking off when it comes to developing skillful qualities.
translation_text.an10.11:2.7: They’re wise. They have the wisdom of arising and passing away which is noble, penetrative, and leads to the complete ending of suffering.
translation_text.an10.11:2.8: That’s how a mendicant has five factors.
translation_text.an10.11:3.1: And how does a lodging have five factors?
translation_text.an10.11:3.2: It’s when a lodging is neither too far nor too near, but convenient for coming and going.
translation_text.an10.11:3.3: It’s not bothered by people by day, and at night it’s quiet and still.
translation_text.an10.11:3.4: There’s little disturbance from flies, mosquitoes, wind, sun, and reptiles.
translation_text.an10.11:3.5: While staying in that lodging the necessities of life—robes, almsfood, lodgings, and medicines and supplies for the sick—are easy to come by.
translation_text.an10.11:3.6: And in that lodging there are several senior mendicants who are very learned, inheritors of the heritage, who have memorized the teachings, the monastic law, and the outlines.
translation_text.an10.11:3.7: From time to time they go up to those mendicants and ask them questions:
translation_text.an10.11:3.8: ‘Why, sir, does it say this? What does that mean?’
translation_text.an10.11:3.9: Those venerables reveal what is hidden, clarify what is unclear, and dispel doubt regarding the many doubtful matters.
translation_text.an10.11:3.10: That’s how a lodging has five factors.
translation_text.an10.11:3.11: A mendicant with five factors, using and frequenting lodgings with five factors, will soon realize the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life, and live having realized it with their own insight due to the ending of defilements.”
